Class AppConfig

java.lang.Object
org.novastack.iposca.config.AppConfig
public class AppConfig extends Object
Class that manages the storage and retrieval of application configurations.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    AppConfig.ConfigKey
    A set of configurations that can be used in IPOS-CA.

    • AppConfig

  • Method Details

    • getKey

      public AppConfig.ConfigKey getKey()
      Getter for the key.
      Returns:
      The key of the configuration which returns AppConfig.ConfigKey

    • getValue

      public byte[] getValue()
      Getter for the value.
      Returns:
      The value of the configuration which returns byte[]

    • configure

      public void configure(AppConfig appConfig)
      Register a configuration into the database (upsert). AppConfig is passed in as a parameter and the config key and the pertaining value are stored in the database. If a configuration key already exists, the value is updated instead. The value is stored as a BLOB, and thus the value must be of type byte[]. The table schema does not impose a NOT NULL constraint as the UI enforces that selectively via TemplatesController and RateController. This method is best used with AppConfigAPI that will encode the primitive values into a byte array.
      Example usage: 
       AppConfig appConfig = new AppConfig(ConfigKey.VAT, AppConfigAPI.encodeInt(10));
appConfig.configure(appConfig);
      Parameters:
      appConfig - The configuration to be stored in the database.

    • get

      public static byte[] get(AppConfig.ConfigKey key)
      Gets a configuration value from the database, with the given configuration key. If the configuration key does not exist, it returns null. This method is best used with AppConfigAPI that will decode the primitive values for use in the application.
      Example usage: 
       int vat = AppConfigAPI.decodeByteToInt(AppConfig.get(AppConfig.ConfigKey.VAT));
      Parameters:
      key - The configuration key to retrieve the value for.
      Returns:
      The value of the configuration key as byte[], or null if the key does not exist.

    • configExists

      public static boolean configExists()
      Checks if a full set of configurations are present in the database. Even if a single configuration is missing, the method returns false. For TemplatesController, a false return value from this function indicates a corrupted configuration. It then prompts the user to re-configure the application.
      Returns:
      boolean to check if the configurations are present in the database.
